async_execution.mechanism: Amp hooks run synchronously; no async execution documented

context_injection.mechanism: Amp hooks cannot inject context into the agent session beyond the pre-execute 'send-user-message' action that cancels the tool call

decision_control.mechanism: Pre-execute 'send-user-message' action cancels the pending tool call and injects a user message; exit code 0 = allow, non-zero = block

handler_types.mechanism: command-only: hook 'action' field runs a shell command; no HTTP, LLM, or agent handler types documented

hook_scopes.mechanism: Project only: hooks live in a single 'amp.hooks' array inside '.amp/settings.json' (or user-scope settings); no multi-scope layering documented

input_modification.mechanism: Amp hooks cannot modify tool input before execution; only substring match and cancel-with-message are documented

json_io_protocol.mechanism: Amp hooks signal decisions via exit codes and declarative action types; no JSON stdin/stdout protocol documented

matcher_patterns.mechanism: Amp hooks filter firing via 'input.contains' — exact literal substring match against tool input

permission_control.mechanism: amp.permissions ordered first-match-wins rule list returns one of allow / reject / ask / delegate for every tool invocation before execution; default fallback is ask on the main thread and reject in sub-agents

providerExtensions.name: JSON merge into amp.hooks array
providerExtensions.summary: Hooks are stored as an array under the 'amp.hooks' key inside '.amp/settings.json' (workspace) or user-scope settings; they are not a standalone hooks file.

providerExtensions.name: Command handler type only
providerExtensions.summary: The hook 'action' field invokes a shell command (declarative pre-execute 'send-user-message' and post-execute 'redact-tool-input' actions); no HTTP, LLM, or agent handler types are documented.

providerExtensions.name: Hook trigger events
providerExtensions.summary: Two events: 'tool:pre-execute' (before tool runs) and 'tool:post-execute' (after); each event accepts only specific actions. Canonical mapping: before_tool_execute → tool:pre-execute, after_tool_execute → tool:post-execute.

providerExtensions.name: Hook action: send-user-message
providerExtensions.summary: Pre-execute action that injects a user message and cancels the pending tool call to enforce behavioral constraints.

providerExtensions.name: Hook action: redact-tool-input
providerExtensions.summary: Post-execute action that redacts tool input fields after execution to avoid storing sensitive arguments.

providerExtensions.name: Hook match condition: input.contains
providerExtensions.summary: Filter hook firing by exact literal substring match in tool input — no regex or glob support.

providerExtensions.name: Hook configuration in settings file
providerExtensions.summary: Hook definitions live as an array in Amp settings files (.amp/settings.json or user-scope settings).

providerExtensions.name: Permissions system (amp.permissions)
providerExtensions.summary: Ordered first-match-wins rule system under 'amp.permissions' with allow/reject/ask/delegate actions and per-context defaults.

providerExtensions.name: Permission match condition types
providerExtensions.summary: Match values support glob patterns ('*'), regex (strings starting and ending with '/'), arrays (OR across entries), literals (boolean/number/null/undefined as exact match), and nested objects (deep property matching with dot notation for objects and numeric strings for array indices). Tool name field also accepts globs (e.g., 'Bash', 'mcp__playwright__*').

providerExtensions.name: Permission delegate action
providerExtensions.summary: Delegate decisions to an external program named in the rule's 'to' field (must be on $PATH or an absolute path). Amp pipes tool parameters as JSON to stdin and exports AMP_THREAD_ID, AGENT_TOOL_NAME, and AGENT=amp env vars. Exit status: 0=allow, 1=ask operator, ≥2=reject (stderr surfaced to the model). Plugin API is the documented successor; a compatibility plugin enforcing amp.permissions rules is planned.

providerExtensions.name: Permission reject with continuation message
providerExtensions.summary: A 'reject' rule may carry a 'message' string returned to the model; when set, the rejection continues the conversation with that feedback instead of halting it.

providerExtensions.name: Default action when no rule matches
providerExtensions.summary: If no user rule and no built-in rule matches, Amp asks the operator on the main thread and rejects the call in sub-agents. Built-in rules (e.g., allowing 'ls' via Bash) are consulted after user rules.

providerExtensions.name: Permission context restriction (thread vs subagent)
providerExtensions.summary: Optionally scopes a rule to main thread, sub-agent only, or both (omitted); defaults differ by context.

providerExtensions.name: Permissions CLI (amp permissions)
providerExtensions.summary: 'amp permissions' subcommands manage rules in a UNIX-shell-style text format without editing JSON: 'list' (with '--builtin' for built-ins only), 'add' (e.g., 'add reject web_search'), 'edit' (opens $EDITOR; also accepts STDIN), and 'test' (dry-runs a tool+args call and prints the matched rule, action, and source). Single/double-quoted strings supported; unquoted true/false/null/numbers parsed as JSON literals; values containing '*' must be quoted.
